_comment: Two adjacent cube faces beveled with group=true. EXPECTED FAIL against MODO: vibe3d's group mode places shared corner verts on the FIRST processed face's inset plane (first-face-wins), while MODO 9 accumulates shifts from BOTH faces at the shared corner — placing it at the geometric intersection of the two inset boundaries (e.g. corner offset 0.15 along +y AND 0.15 along +z, not just along +y). Vibe3D's behavior matches my Blender-side manual replication (see blender_diff/cases/poly_bevel_two_faces_grouped.json) but NOT MODO's actual region-inset geometry. Closing this XFAIL would require implementing accumulated-shift at shared corners, similar to Blender's `bmesh.ops.inset_region`.
